description: Trigger discrimination for the compliance cluster: the org-level voluntary RMF program vs per-feature review, the certifiable AIMS, feature threat modeling, eval execution, and the SDLC contract.

cases.prompt: Operationalize GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE across our AI systems' lifecycle — owners, triggers, and a risk register.
cases.reason: Standing up the RMF functions as an org-level lifecycle program is the lifecycle manager; feature reviews feed it and the AIMS wraps it.

cases.prompt: Before this AI feature ships, determine its risk tier and whether it needs human-in-the-loop oversight.
cases.reason: Per-feature tiering and oversight matching is the Phase 7 reviewer; the program aggregates such reviews as MAP intake.

cases.prompt: We want to be certified for how we manage AI — build the management system an auditor can certify.
cases.reason: Certification is ISO 42001's AIMS; the RMF is a voluntary method that cannot be certified against.

cases.prompt: Enumerate the injection, disclosure, and tool-abuse threats for our RAG assistant and rank them by exploit path.
cases.reason: Feature-level AI threat enumeration is the Phase 7 modeler; the program consumes its output during MAP.

cases.prompt: Build and run the eval suite with safety and grounding thresholds gating our releases.
cases.reason: Designing/running evals is the manual-only harness; the program only wires its thresholds into MEASURE.

cases.prompt: Write the operating model for how humans and agents move work through stages with gates and authority.
cases.reason: The SDLC stage/authority contract is the Phase 1.5 skill; the RMF program maps it as GOVERN machinery.
